_meta.name: Validation Chapter, Test #1: substrate behavior against a fresh-session question battery
_meta.purpose: Snapshot what the substrate surfaces, blocks, and grounds for typical user questions. Each question hits one of the substrate's read tools (aether_search, aether_sanction, aether_fidelity) with no LLM in the loop, so the result is deterministic given the substrate state.
_meta.scope: Run against the live ~/.aether/mcp_state.json. Re-run after substrate evolves to diff behavior over time.
_meta.future_work: Compare with-substrate vs no-substrate (control), expand to N>1 user, and grade against ground-truth labels.

categories.name: Memory recall
categories.what_to_look_for: Substrate should surface specific facts the user previously stated, ranked by trust and similarity. A no-substrate baseline returns nothing; the substrate should return populated results with trust=0.85+ for confidently-stated facts.

questions.expectation: Substrate has 9 distinct values (post-merge). All 7 trust=0.95 truths should rank above any trust<0.7 demoted entries (F#10 contract).

Substrate has 6 employer values inherited from production. Multi-employer is real (left a design studio, currently freelance) — search should return them all so the model can render the history accurately.

categories.name: Contradiction handling
categories.what_to_look_for: Substrate should expose held-but-not-resolved tensions instead of silently picking one. A no-substrate baseline can't even know there is a conflict.

questions.expectation: Substrate has 15 distinct occupation values: real (freelance dev, web developer) + LLM hallucinations (stocking, filmmaker, research engineer) + user-trolling (dork, fucking dork). Search should return them; downstream, slot-equality detection should flag the conflict.
Substrate has 9 names: truth (Nick, Nick Block) + LLM hallucinations (Aether, Claude, Jake, Marcus, October Baby, Turbo, World). Same shape as B1.

categories.name: Sanction gate (action governance)
categories.what_to_look_for: Substrate's aether_sanction should auto-ground in beliefs and return APPROVE / HOLD / REJECT. High-trust contradicting memories force REJECT. A no-substrate baseline cannot block anything based on belief state.

questions.expectation: Substrate has policy beliefs around force-push (F#4 fix). Should return HOLD or REJECT depending on belief trust.
Even without a specific belief, governance layer's structural checks should HOLD this destructive language.

categories.name: Fidelity grounding
categories.what_to_look_for: aether_fidelity scores a draft response against substrate grounding. Drafts that contradict held beliefs get a contradicting count > 0 and a low belief_confidence. A no-substrate baseline cannot detect the contradiction.

questions.draft: Your favorite color is definitively orange.
questions.expectation: Substrate has multi-color belief (orange is one of 9). Should ground partially but not strongly contradict — orange is supported with trust=0.95 but isn't the unique answer.

questions.draft: You work at Stripe and have for three years.
questions.expectation: Substrate's employer set does not include Stripe. Fidelity should flag this as low-confidence / contradicting.

categories.name: Cold queries (no relevant memory)
categories.what_to_look_for: Substrate should NOT pretend to know things it doesn't. Search returns ~empty (no high-similarity matches). Fidelity returns low belief_confidence. The point: the substrate's signal is honest about absence.

questions.expectation: No relevant memory; results should be empty or low-similarity.

questions.draft: The speed of light is 299,792,458 m/s in vacuum.
questions.expectation: No grounding in substrate; belief_confidence should be low. The model can still make this claim, but the substrate honestly reports it has no opinion.
